board.title: Human-Computer Interaction
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: The term Human-Computer Interaction was popularized by the 1983 book "The Psychology of Human–Computer Interaction." It's now an established field at the intersection of computer science, behavioral science, and design.

clues.question: Design principle where essential functions and system state are apparent to users
Predictive model where decision time increases logarithmically with number of choices; models selecting from a menu
Elements that restrict a user's possible actions; include physical, semantic, and logical elements
Usability researcher who defined the 10 heuristics used to evaluate user interfaces; emphasized users expect interfaces to match prior experiences
Cues that indicate a user's possible actions and how to perform them
Possible actions readily perceived by users based on context and prior experience
System response that informs users of actions, results, and state; reduces the gulf of evaluation
Researcher at Xerox PARC who co-authored "The Psychology of Human-Computer Interaction" and developed models like GOMS and the Model Human Processor
Relationship between controls and their effects
Delay between a user's action and system response
Cognitive scientist who formalized user-centered design in "User-Centered System Design" and popularized concepts like affordances and signifiers
CS scientist who defined direct manipulation interfaces, letting users interact with visible objects; developed Eight Golden Rules of Interface Design
Current condition of a system that determines its behavior and user interactions
Predictive model where time to reach a target depends on distance and size; models pointing to on-screen targets
